exemplar.title:
How do the chemical components in different shampoos and
conditioners (Kerastase Genesis, Loreal Paris, Fable and Mane), affect the tensile strength (g) of Homo sapiens hair when wet and dry?

How do the chemical components in different shampoos and
conditioners (Kerastase Genesis, Loreal Paris, Fable and Mane), affect the tensile strength (g) of Homo sapiens hair when wet and dry?

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question clearly stated and identifiable
Topic communicated adequately with sufficient background context
Methodology described in detail with relevant controls and ethical considerations
weaknesses: Explanation remains largely descriptive with limited critical elaboration of biochemical mechanisms
Research question scope is broad (three brands, two moisture states) limiting focus on specific components
Method relies on improvised apparatus with high uncertainty and lacks calibration justification
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands (topic communication, research question focus, methodology appropriateness) fall in the 3â4 band. The student provides an adequate overview and justifies experimental steps, but explanations are descriptive rather than comprehensive, focus is only partially realised, and apparatus limitations are not fully addressed. These characteristics merit a mid-band score of 4 out of 6.

strengths: Background section synthesises information from a wide range of sources
Terminology (e.g., keratin, surfactant, tensile strength) is mostly accurate
weaknesses: Application of source material is largely descriptive with minimal critical appraisal
Key mechanistic claims are not supported by primary research or detailed source evaluation
Inconsistent formatting of taxonomy and units reduces precision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates clear knowledge of core concepts and uses appropriate terminology (band 3â4) but applies source material in a predominantly descriptive manner, lacking analytical integration (band 1â2). Overall, the performance sits between these bands, justifying a score of 3 out of 6.

strengths: Competent processing of data into means, standard deviations and use of ANOVA
Evaluation section identifies methodological limitations and suggests reasonable improvements
Argument is structured logically with links back to the research question
weaknesses: Analysis remains surface-level, without probing underlying mechanisms
Statistical interpretations contain inaccuracies (e.g., non-significant Tukey claims)
Argument includes over-generalisations and occasional inconsistencies
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each of the three critical thinking strandsâanalysis, discussion and evaluation, argument developmentâis placed at the lower end of the 4â6 band. The student shows adequate competence and coherence but lacks depth and precision, warranting a score of 4 out of 12.

strengths: Report follows a clear and appropriate overall structure
All required elements (title page, TOC, sections) are present and logically organised
Figures and tables are generally placed close to relevant text
weaknesses: Reference and formatting styles are inconsistent
Figure/table numbering and captions contain sequencing and descriptive errors
Layout lapses (indented TOC, inconsistent significant figures) detract from presentation quality
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The structure strand is solid (band 3â4) but layout and formatting contain several inconsistencies (band 1â2). On balance, the presentation merits a mid-band score of 3 out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The report contains all required structural elementsâtitle page with word count, abstract, table of contents, introduction, method, results, discussion, evaluation, bibliography and appendices. Headings and sub-headings allow the reader to follow the study without difficulty, and figures/tables are generally placed close to relevant text.  
Minor issues exist (e.g., some duplicated figure captions, lengthy background sections disrupting balance, occasional redundancy), but overall **the structure is clear and appropriate throughout**, warranting placement in the 3-4 band (score 3).

Essential formal elements are present: title page, table of contents, section headings, numbered tables/figures and stated word count. Page numbering is visible in the table of contents, and most visuals have captions.  
However, **reference formatting is inconsistent** (mix of MLA-style URLs, missing access dates, varied capitalisation), some figure captions are duplicated, and a few tables/figures run across pages without clear formatting. These lapses indicate that layout considerations are only partially met, aligning with the 1-2 band (score 2).

The essay follows a logical sequence (background â method â data â analysis â conclusion) and each section attempts to relate back to the research question, demonstrating **a coherent line of reasoning**. Claims are generally supported by data summaries, and the student returns to hypotheses in the conclusion.  
Nonetheless, the argument suffers from **inconsistencies and over-generalisations**: explanations of statistical results are sometimes contradictory, and the leap from observed mass differences to molecular mechanisms is not fully justified. The argument therefore retains coherence but lacks sustained depth, positioning it in the 4-6 band with a score of 4.

The student states the topic, title and research question prominently on the title page and introduces the biological context of hair structure and cosmetic chemistry in the introduction. **Key concepts such as keratin architecture, surfactants and tensile strength are outlined with supporting references, giving the reader a workable overview of why the investigation matters.**  
However, the explanation remains largely descriptive; links between the chosen brands, their advertised claims and the specific biochemical mechanisms are asserted rather than critically elaborated. Some repetition and minor taxonomic inaccuracies (e.g. *Homo Sapien* instead of *Homo sapiens*) detract from precision. Overall, the topic is **adequately communicated with sufficientâthough not fully comprehensiveâexplanation**, fitting the 3-4 band.

A long bibliography demonstrates that the student consulted numerous secondary sources and cites them in text; the background section synthesises information on hair structure and product ingredients. **However, the use of this material in later sections is largely descriptive and illustrative rather than analytical.**  
Key claims about how specific ingredients (e.g. antioxidants, silicones) influence tensile strength are presented without critical appraisal of source quality, currency or experimental evidence. Primary research literature is cited but rarely interrogated, and there is minimal integration of source data into the interpretation of results. The limited relevance and mostly descriptive application of sources place this strand in the 1-2 band, with a score of 2.

The student processes raw data into means, standard deviations and employs two-way ANOVA followed by Tukey HSD, indicating **a competent attempt at statistical analysis**. Graphs and tables allow patterns (e.g., wet vs dry differences) to be identified and described.  
However, the analysis remains surface-level: explanations often repeat observable trends without probing underlying biochemical mechanisms; misinterpretation of statistical outcomes (e.g., claiming significance from non-significant Tukey results) shows limited critical depth. Uncertainties are acknowledged but not propagated into final conclusions. These features denote **adequate but partial relevance and rigour**, meriting the 4-6 band at the lower end (score 4).

The student adopts an accessible tensile-loading set-up adapted from literature and lists variables, controls and safety/ethical considerations. **Procedural steps, control variables and justification of method are presented in detail, showing awareness of experimental design basics.**  
Yet, the method depends on improvised apparatus (paper-clip masses, kitchen scale) with relatively large uncertainties (Â±1 g) and only five replicates per treatment, limiting reliability. Selection of brands appears driven more by availability than clear scientific criteria, and no pilot study or calibration data are provided. The range of methods is generally relevant but evidence of *effective* selection and optimisation is limited, so the work fits the 3-4 band.

The conclusion restates key findings and attempts to link statistical outcomes to product claims, and an evaluation section identifies several methodological limitations with sensible improvements. **Strengths, weaknesses and future extensions are explicitly discussed, showing some evaluative thinking.**  
Despite this, the evaluation is fairly generic, with limited depth on how specific errors quantitatively affect data or how proposed improvements would change reliability. Conclusions sometimes exceed the evidence (e.g., attributing brand performance to ingredient classes without direct chemical analysis). Thus the discussion is **good in structure but only moderately critical**, fitting the lower end of the 4-6 band (score 4).

The research question is explicitly stated and easily identifiable: it asks how the chemical components of three commercial shampoo/conditioner lines influence the tensile strength of wet and dry human hair. **Clarity is satisfactory and the variables (product type and moisture condition) are recognisable.**  
Nevertheless, the scope is rather broadâencompassing three brands, six treatment modes and two moisture statesâso the focus on cdchemical componentscd is only partially realised; the essay ultimately compares brands more than individual chemicals. This partial focus aligns with the 3-4 band.

Throughout the essay the student employs biological terms such as cdkeratincd, cdintermediate filamentcd, cdsurfactantcd and cdtensile strengthcd with reasonable accuracy, and chemical nomenclature for common ingredients is generally correct. **Most explanations demonstrate a sound grasp of core concepts at a Diploma-level.**  
Nonetheless, there are recurrent lapsesâe.g. inconsistent use of the species name (*Homo Sapien* vs *Homo sapiens*), confusion between mass and force units, and occasional informal phrasingâwhich detract from consistency and precision. This "mostly accurate" but not flawless usage aligns with the 3-4 band, score 3.

annotations.comment: Figure 4 caption âPreparation of hairâ is too brief; expand to describe key steps and parameters shown in the image.
Uncertainties are tabulated but not propagated through calculations; integrate uncertainty analysis into data processing to strengthen conclusions.
Mean and standard deviation tables use inconsistent significant figures; standardize decimal places across all data presentations.
Sentence fragment âKeratin's ability to switch from alpha toâ is incomplete; revise for clarity and ensure completeness.
Descriptions of the experimental method lack explicit linkage to the research question; clarify how tensile strength measurements directly test your hypotheses.
Title is overly generic and doesnât reflect specific variables or scope; revise to include key factors (product brands, moisture states) for clarity.
Word count is within the limit, demonstrating adherence to the formatting requirement.
Table of contents uses leader dots inconsistently and levels are not clearly indented; adopt uniform indentation to improve navigation.
âTABLE OF CONTENTSâ title uses uppercase and underline formatting which is visually heavy; consider simpler heading style to enhance readability.
Research question is clearly stated but overly broad by encompassing three brands and two moisture conditions; consider narrowing to enhance depth of analysis.
The term âHomo Sapienâ is incorrectly capitalized and not fully italicized; correct to Homo sapiens to maintain taxonomic accuracy.
Background is descriptive but does not connect structural details (medulla, cortex) to expected tensile strength outcomes; integrate relevance to the investigation question.
Figure 1 is informative but lacks in-text discussion of its key features; directly refer to this hierarchical structure in your analysis to strengthen integration.
Units for tensile strength are given as â150-260 Mpaâ; correct to MPa and ensure consistent SI formatting throughout.
Figure numbering is inconsistent (Figure 3 appears before Figure 2); renumber figures sequentially to avoid confusion.
Methodology justification acknowledges limitations but does not discuss effectiveness of improvised apparatus relative to standard equipment; elaborate on trade-offs.
Independent variables section lists âShampoo and Conditionerâ generically; specify brand names and combinations to improve operational definitions.
Equipment list includes â1 scale (Â±1)â without unit designation; specify grams and ensure consistent uncertainty notation.
Equipment list uses inconsistent notation; âÂ±1â should be clearly linked to a unit (g) and styled consistently with other uncertainties.
Marketing claims are presented without critical evaluation; appraise source quality and existing literature to demonstrate analytical depth.
Product ingredient descriptions rely on marketing sources; include peer-reviewed or primary research to substantiate mechanistic claims.
Transition from shampoo to conditioner is abrupt; add a brief justification for separate testing to clarify experimental design rationale.
Decision to report tensile strength in grams instead of SI units (Pascals) is not justified; discuss implications for comparability and interpretation.
Control variables table is comprehensive but does not quantify the magnitude of each factorâs potential variation; consider estimating impacts on measurements.
Method justification notes environmental controls but lacks detail on how humidity was measured; include instrument data for reproducibility.
Assembly figure lacks scale or dimension reference; include measurements (book height, gap) in the image or caption for clarity.
Safety risk table omits mention of PPE (e.g., gloves, goggles) for chemical handling; explicitly list required protective equipment.
Graph 1 lacks clear axis labels (units, category descriptions); revise to make the chart self-contained and interpretable.
Interpretation restates results without linking back explicitly to the research question; discuss how findings address your hypotheses.
Choice of Î± = 0.005 for ANOVA is unconventional and unexplained; justify alpha selection or align with standard thresholds.
Hypotheses table reverses null and alternative definitions for interaction terms; ensure correct logical statements and explanations.
Tukey HSD interpretation claims no significance despite ANOVA detecting differences; discuss potential causes (e.g., power, effect size) to reconcile results.
Conclusion restates findings but lacks discussion of practical implications for consumer choice or product formulation; consider adding real-world relevance.
Limitation âProcedural Restraintsâ suggests professional equipment is needed; propose pilot testing to quantify apparatus variability.
Table numbering jumps to â18â without preceding sequence; ensure sequential numbering of tables throughout to maintain coherence.
Bibliography format is inconsistent (mix of styles); adopt a single citation format and apply it uniformly across all references.
Multiple online sources lack access dates; include access dates for web references to satisfy citation standards.
